name: South Dakota SNAP
description: South Dakota's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to low-income households to purchase groceries. Administered by the South Dakota Department of Social Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: South Dakota Medicaid
description: South Dakota's Medicaid program providing health coverage including doctor visits, hospital stays, prescriptions, and behavioral health services for eligible low-income residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults 19-64: up to 138% FPL (expansion effective July 2024). Children: up to 204% FPL.

name: South Dakota Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to South Dakota workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the SD Department of Labor and Regulation.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own and meet wage and work requirements

name: South Dakota Housing Development Authority (SDHDA)
description: South Dakota's housing finance agency providing affordable housing programs including rental assistance, homebuyer programs, down payment assistance, and housing development.
eligibilitySummary: Low-to-moderate income South Dakota residents seeking housing assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program and county; generally at or below 80% area median income

name: East River Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents in eastern South Dakota in areas including housing, family law, public benefits, consumer issues, and Native American law.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of eastern South Dakota with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Dakota Plains Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents in central and western South Dakota and tribal reservations, covering housing, family law, benefits, and Indian law issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of western/central South Dakota and tribal reservation residents
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: South Dakota Department of Labor and Regulation - Job Service
description: Provides workforce development services through local job service offices including job search assistance, career counseling, resume help, training programs, and employer connections.
eligibilitySummary: All South Dakota residents seeking employment or career development services

name: South Dakota LIHEAP (Low Income Energy Assistance Program)
description: Helps low-income South Dakota households pay heating costs during winter months. Provides seasonal heating assistance and emergency energy crisis intervention.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income South Dakota households with heating costs
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 150% of federal poverty level

name: Helpline Center 211 South Dakota
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting South Dakotans to local health and human services including food, housing, utilities, healthcare, and crisis intervention.
eligibilitySummary: All South Dakota residents seeking community resource information

name: Feeding South Dakota
description: The state's Feeding America affiliate food bank providing food distribution through mobile food pantries, BackPack programs, and over 250 partner agencies across all 66 South Dakota counties.
eligibilitySummary: Any South Dakota resident experiencing food insecurity

name: South Dakota Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Provides services to South Dakota veterans including benefits claims assistance, employment services, education benefits, and connections to VA healthcare and state veterans homes.
eligibilitySummary: South Dakota veterans and their eligible dependents

name: South Dakota Crisis Line (988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
description: Statewide crisis hotline providing immediate support for mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, and emotional distress. Connects callers to local crisis services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in South Dakota experiencing a mental health crisis or emotional distress

name: South Dakota Child Care Assistance
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income families pay for childcare so parents can work or attend education and training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Working families with children under 13 meeting income and employment requirements
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 175% of federal poverty level at initial eligibility

name: South Dakota TANF
description: Cash assistance program for low-income South Dakota families with dependent children, providing monthly benefits and work-related services to help achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children meeting work participation requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; very low thresholds

name: South Dakota WIC
description: Provides nutrition education, breastfeeding support, healthy food benefits, and healthcare referrals for low-income pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: South Dakota Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements to low-income South Dakota households including insulation, air sealing, and heating system upgrades to reduce utility costs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: South Dakota Division of Rehabilitation Services
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation services to South Dakotans with disabilities including career planning, skills training, assistive technology, and job placement assistance.
eligibilitySummary: South Dakota residents with disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: South Dakota Adult Education and GED
description: Provides free adult basic education, GED preparation, and ESL classes for South Dakota adults seeking to improve education and employment readiness.
eligibilitySummary: South Dakota adults age 16 and older not enrolled in school

name: South Dakota Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for survivors of domestic violence in South Dakota.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence in South Dakota

name: Sioux Falls Area Community Action (Sioux Empire United Way)
description: Provides community action services to the Sioux Falls area including energy assistance, food programs, financial coaching, and connections to community resources.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of the greater Sioux Falls area

name: Sioux Area Metro (SAM) Transit
description: Public transit service for the Sioux Falls metropolitan area providing fixed-route bus service and paratransit. Reduced fares for seniors, youth, and persons with disabilities.
eligibilitySummary: All Sioux Falls area residents; reduced fares available for qualifying groups

name: Inter-Lakes Community Action Partnership
description: Community action agency serving central South Dakota providing LIHEAP, weatherization, Head Start, housing assistance, and food programs for low-income families.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of central South Dakota counties

name: Rapid City Collective Impact
description: Coordinates community resources and services in Rapid City to reduce homelessness and address poverty, connecting individuals to housing, employment, and supportive services.
eligibilitySummary: Rapid City residents experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness

name: South Dakota Head Start
description: Comprehensive early childhood education for low-income South Dakota families providing education, health screenings, nutrition, and family support for children birth to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children birth to age 5 and pregnant women
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 100% of federal poverty level; categorical eligibility for certain groups

name: South Dakota Rural Transit
description: Provides public transit services in rural communities across South Dakota through a network of local providers, helping residents access medical appointments, shopping, and employment.
eligibilitySummary: Rural South Dakota residents, particularly seniors and persons with disabilities

name: South Dakota Disability Rights
description: The state's protection and advocacy system providing free legal services to people with disabilities on issues including access to services, abuse and neglect, and civil rights.
eligibilitySummary: South Dakota residents with disabilities facing rights violations or needing legal advocacy

name: South Dakota Veterans Bonus Program
description: South Dakota provides a state bonus payment to eligible veterans who served during specific periods of conflict. Also administers the state veterans cemetery and memorial programs.
eligibilitySummary: South Dakota veterans who served during eligible periods of conflict and meet residency requirements

name: Great Plains Tribal Leaders Health Board
description: Provides healthcare advocacy and services for tribal communities in South Dakota including health promotion, disease prevention, behavioral health, and community health programs.
eligibilitySummary: Native American residents of South Dakota tribal communities

name: Sioux Falls Banquet (Community Meals)
description: Free community meals program in Sioux Falls serving hot dinners seven nights a week to anyone in need, no questions asked. Also provides sack lunches and hygiene supplies.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of a meal in the Sioux Falls area

name: South Dakota Housing for the Homeless Consortium
description: Statewide consortium coordinating homelessness prevention, rapid re-housing, and permanent supportive housing programs funded through HUD Continuum of Care grants.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of homelessness
